message:
fix(daemon): suppress bogus predictions, fix wrong mount in state/logs

Five bugs fixed in the daemon monitoring loop:

1. check_predictive_warning() had no confidence gate — emitted "disk
   full in N min" warnings at 0% confidence on healthy disks.  Now
   suppresses predictions below config min_confidence (default 70%).

2. check_predictive_warning() was missing the Orange severity tier —
   jumped from Warning directly to Red.  A drop from 25 min to 8 min
   remaining would not re-notify (both mapped to Warning).  Added
   Orange tier using action_horizon_minutes, matching the 4-tier
   classification in NotificationEvent::PredictiveWarning::level().

3. Predictive warning state (last_predictive_level, last_predictive_warning)
   was never reset when predictions cleared.  Hours later when pressure
   returned, the stale severity could suppress the first new warning.
   Now resets both fields when predicted_seconds is None.

4. State file and pressure-change logs used primary_path() for free_pct
   and mount name, but response.level came from the worst mount.  This
   showed "Red pressure, 30% free" when a secondary mount was at 2%.
   Now uses response.causing_mount for both.

5. Special location warnings were sent as NotificationEvent::Error
   (Red level), which bypasses the global notification throttle —
   firing every 5 seconds.  Changed to PressureChanged with the
   computed pressure level so the throttle applies.

Also fixed: self_monitor.record_deletions() now updates last_scan_deleted
so the state file reflects actual deletions (was always 0 because scan
and deletion completions arrive via separate WorkerReport variants).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
